title: TARP tunnel component, Lower Des Plaines : environmental impact statement.

schema.page_numbers: ESTIMATED from char offsets at 2500 chars/page.
schema.merge_rule: One row per (entity, stance) — stance changes produce separate rows.
schema.cost_note: USD costs are ESTIMATES from settings.PRICES_USD_PER_M; verify against AWS Bedrock invoice.

entries.summary_quote: Maintenance costs can either be covered through an ad valorem property tax, or through a user charge system based on water consumption. EPA favors the latter approach and has awarded the MSDGC two grants to develop such a user charge system.

mentions.quote: Maintenance costs can either be covered through an ad valorem property tax, or through a user charge system based on water consumption. EPA favors the latter approach and has awarded the MSDGC two grants to develop such a user charge system.
mentions.stance_basis: EPA favors user charge system over ad valorem tax for O&M financing

rubric_results.check: Summary quote present verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The cited page 15 text contains no mention of EPA, ad valorem property tax, user charge system, water consumption, or MSDGC grants. The quote is entirely absent from the provided text.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links EPA to the stated stance
rubric_results.note: EPA is not mentioned anywhere in the cited page text; no paraphrased attribution can be verified.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: Cannot verify stance as the relevant text is not present in the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: EPA is a standard, well-formed agency abbreviation.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The cited text does not mention EPA or its role as an administering agency in this context.

critic.notes: The summary quote and all associated content about EPA, user charge systems, and MSDGC grants are completely absent from the cited page 15 text. The page text discusses the FCCC's plan selection process and TARP development. The correct page reference needs to be identified and the extraction re-done with the proper citation.

entries.role: intergovernmental coordinating committee evaluating alternative plans
entries.summary_quote: In November 1970, the Flood Control Coordinating Committee (FCCC) was reactivated to review and evaluate the plans developed and formulate additional ones to address both the flooding and water pollution problems.

mentions.quote: These alternatives are less costly and more environmentally acceptable to the community than any of the other plans presented. Detail studies along the lines of these alternatives should proceed to develop the final plan layout.
mentions.stance_basis: FCCC unanimously recommended TARP-type underflow plans as best alternative
mentions.role_as_written: Intergovernmental committee evaluating flood/pollution control plans

mentions.quote: In November 1970, the Flood Control Coordinating Committee (FCCC) was reactivated to review and evaluate the plans developed and formulate additional ones to address both the flooding and water pollution problems.
mentions.stance_basis: Evaluated and recommended TARP through systematic elimination of alternatives

mentions.quote: These alternatives are less costly and more environmentally acceptable to the community than any of the other plans presented. Detail studies along the lines of these alternatives should proceed to develop the final plan layout.
mentions.stance_basis: FCCC unanimously concluded Chicago Underflow plans were best and recommended TARP development
mentions.role_as_written: intergovernmental coordinating committee evaluating alternative plans

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact quote about FCCC being 'reactivated in November 1970 to review and evaluate the plans...' does not appear in the pulled text from pages 18, 140, or 340. The cited pages discuss TARP tunnel systems, costs, and FCCC conclusions but not the reactivation sentence.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links FCCC to stated stance
rubric_results.note: The cited text does mention FCCC concluding that TARP had the highest ranking and was selected as the most suitable plan, supporting an in_favor stance toward TARP.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The FCCC selected TARP as the most suitable plan, which is consistent with an in_favor stance.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: Flood Control Coordinating Committee (FCCC) is an official organizational name and is properly formatted.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The cited text confirms FCCC analyzed and ranked alternative plans, supporting the role of 'intergovernmental coordinating committee evaluating alternative plans.'

critic.notes: The summary quote about FCCC being 'reactivated in November 1970' is not present verbatim in any of the cited pages provided. While the stance (in_favor) and entity are well-supported by the text that does appear (FCCC selected TARP as the highest-ranked plan), the specific summary quote needs to be re-verified against the correct page. The extractor should locate the exact page where the reactivation sentence appears and update the evidence_pages accordingly.

The stance 'in_favor' is not supported — EPA's role here is procedural/regulatory (grant review, EIS preparation), not advocacy for the project. The entity and role are correctly identified, but the stance label and quote need correction; EPA's stance would be better characterized as 'neutral' or the row should reflect its administrative function rather than project support. [Forced HUMAN_REVIEW: quote not verbatim.]
